text: Early childhood development nurturing curiosity, creativity, and essential social skills through play-based learning in a safe and loving environment.
Building strong foundations in literacy, numeracy, and moral values — developing well-rounded, disciplined learners ready to excel and lead.
Rigorous academics, critical thinking, and leadership development — preparing students for national exams, higher education, and global careers.
Across all levels, St. Lawrence Academy is committed to nurturing South Sudanese generations to become
responsible, confident, and globally minded leaders
Empowering the next generation of South Sudanese leaders through excellence, innovation, and character development.
Rigorous curriculum following South Sudan National Standards with proven outstanding results and university preparation.
Modern teaching methods, digital learning tools, and technology integration preparing students for the future.
Secure campus with caring staff where every student feels valued, supported, and empowered to grow confidently.
Strong moral values, leadership development, discipline, and integrity shaping responsible future citizens.
Celebrating South Sudanese heritage while preparing students for global opportunities and international success.
Comprehensive athletics programs, music, drama, and creative arts developing well-rounded talented individuals.
Active engagement in community projects teaching compassion, responsibility, and social awareness to students.
Professional counseling, university placement support, and career planning ensuring bright futures for graduates.
Take a tour of our facilities and learning spaces
Our state-of-the-art classrooms are equipped with modern teaching aids and comfortable seating arrangements designed to foster interactive learning and student engagement.
Our expansive school compound provides a safe and secure environment for learning and growth with well-maintained grounds, green spaces, and modern infrastructure.
Our comprehensive sports facilities include basketball courts, football fields, and athletic tracks developing well-rounded students through physical education and competitive sports.
Our large assembly ground serves as the heart of our school community, hosting morning assemblies, cultural events, sports days, and special celebrations.
Discover the vibrant community and enriching experiences that make St. Lawrence Academy special
Competitive sports programs including basketball, football, volleyball, and athletics developing teamwork and physical fitness.
Student council, prefect system, and leadership training programs building confident future leaders.
Music, drama, dance, and cultural celebrations fostering creativity and appreciation for diverse traditions.
Debate club, science club, math club, and more fostering intellectual curiosity and academic excellence.
Volunteer programs and outreach initiatives teaching compassion, responsibility, and social awareness.
Career guidance, mentorship programs, and university preparation supporting students' future success.
Simple steps to join the St. Lawrence Academy family
Schedule a campus tour, meet our staff, and learn about our programmes and admission requirements.
Fill out the application form with student information, academic history, and parent/guardian details.
Student takes a placement test and attends an interview to determine the appropriate grade level.
Receive your acceptance letter, submit required documents, pay fees, and complete registration.

St. Lawrence Academy, Juba, South Sudan
Achier John: The Man Who Dared to Build
From a dream to a thriving institution — the story of how St. Lawrence Academy came to be, told through the defining moments that shaped its founding.
The principles that guide Achier John's leadership and shape everything at St. Lawrence Academy.
Every student deserves a rigorous, inspiring education that challenges them to reach their full potential. Mediocrity is never acceptable when our students' futures are at stake.
Academic achievement means little without integrity, compassion, and responsibility. St. Lawrence Academy forms complete human beings — scholars and servants of society.
Every graduate carries a responsibility to give back. Our mission is inseparable from the development and flourishing of South Sudan as a nation.
Education does not happen in isolation. Families, teachers, and the wider community must all be partners in the journey of every child who walks through our gates.
The world changes, and education must change with it. We constantly seek better ways to teach, to engage, and to prepare students for tomorrow.
Rooted in faith, St. Lawrence Academy nurtures students who are grounded in strong values — students who act with honour, lead with humility, and live with purpose.
Established in 2020, serving Nursery through Secondary education in Juba, South Sudan.
Students who have gone on to contribute meaningfully to South Sudanese society and beyond.
Recruited and developed a team of dedicated, qualified educators committed to excellence.
Expanding facilities continuously to meet the needs of a growing and thriving student body.
Achier John — Founder & Director, St. Lawrence Academy

St. Lawrence Academy — Juba, South Sudan

Guided Through Play, Prepared for Life
A gentle, nurturing introduction to school life — building confidence, basic communication, and a love of learning through play and guided exploration.
Children grow in independence and begin more structured learning. Language deepens, early number concepts are introduced, and social bonds strengthen.
Top class prepares children for Primary One. Learning becomes more structured — focusing on reading readiness, early writing, and mathematical thinking.
Pre-writing skills and fine motor control
Speaking, listening, and early conversation skills — the foundation of all academic learning.
Letters, sounds, phonics awareness, and a love of books built through stories and guided reading.
Numbers, counting, shapes, patterns, and early mathematical thinking through play.
Drawing, painting, craft, singing, and movement that nurture imagination and self-expression.
Gross and fine motor skills built through structured play, outdoor activities, and physical exercises.
Confidence, empathy, cooperation, and emotional wellbeing developed in a safe and caring environment.
Exploring nature, community, weather, and the world — building curiosity and early scientific thinking.
Stories and activities that instil honesty, kindness, respect, and care for others.
Arrival, free play, and morning circle — greetings, songs, and the day's theme introduction.
Guided learning activities — literacy, numeracy, and creative arts sessions in small groups.
Snack time and outdoor play — physical development, fresh air, and free social interaction.
Story time, music, or a special activity — followed by reflection and tidy-up routines.
Rest period, independent play, and afternoon activities before dismissal.
Small class sizes ensure every child receives individual attention and care from their teacher every single day.
A fully supervised, child-safe space where children can explore and thrive without distraction or risk.
Activities are designed around how young children learn best — through play, curiosity, and guided discovery.
Our nursery teachers are trained in early childhood education and deeply committed to every child in their care.
Daily routines give children a sense of security and predictability, helping them settle, focus, and feel at home.
We keep parents informed and involved — through written reports, parent meetings, and open, honest communication.
A look at the warmth, energy, and joy of everyday life in our nursery.
Give Your Child the Best Possible Start
Nursery children at St. Lawrence Academy
Children during a typical nursery day
Eight Years That Shape a Lifetime
Rigorous Teaching in a Caring Environment
Reading, writing, grammar, comprehension, and oral communication. English is the language of instruction and a core subject built across all eight primary years.
Number work, arithmetic, fractions, geometry, measurement, and problem-solving built through structured and practical learning throughout all primary years.
Exploring the natural world through observation — living things, the human body, materials, forces, and basic environmental science taught progressively.
Geography, history, civics, and culture — helping students understand their community, country, and the world as responsible, informed citizens.
Bible stories, Christian values, prayer, and moral education — nurturing faith, character, and the ethical principles that guide students through life.
Enrol Your Child in Primary School
Primary students at St. Lawrence Academy
Two Streams, One Standard of Excellence
For students with a passion for the natural world, medicine, engineering, and technology.
The study of living organisms, genetics, ecology, and the human body.
Atoms, elements, reactions, and materials through theory and laboratory practicals.
Forces, energy, electricity, waves, and motion — analytical and practical.
Advanced topics for students targeting science and engineering careers.
Crop production, animal husbandry, soil science, and sustainable farming.
ICT skills, digital literacy, and introductory programming.
For students drawn to business, law, humanities, and social sciences.
Trade, business operations, marketing, and economic systems.
African, world, and South Sudanese history and its shaping of modern societies.
Physical and human geography — landforms, climate, population, and development.
Bookkeeping, financial statements, and business accounts.
Prose, poetry, drama, and literary analysis developing critical thinking.
Regardless of stream, every secondary student studies these four subjects.
Algebra, geometry, trigonometry, and statistics — the foundation of many career paths and a core examination subject.
Advanced reading, writing, comprehension, and oral communication — essential for academic success and professional life.
In-depth study of the Bible, Christian doctrine, and ethics — encouraging personal reflection and moral development.
Rights, responsibilities, governance, and national identity — preparing informed, active citizens of South Sudan.
The South Sudan Certificate of Secondary Education
Mock examinations and past paper practice
Begin Your Secondary Journey at SLA
Secondary students at St. Lawrence Academy
Built for Every Stage of Learning
Learning through play, guided by eight developmental domains — language, literacy, numeracy, creativity, physical development, social skills, environmental awareness, and values.
Five core subjects — English, Mathematics, Science, Social Studies, and Christian Religious Education — taught progressively across all primary years, leading to the PLE.
Sciences and Arts streams with a shared core — 15 subjects in total, taught by subject specialists, culminating in the SSCSE national examination.
Rigorous, knowledge-rich teaching that builds deep understanding across all subjects.
Honesty, respect, and integrity woven into daily school life, not treated as extras.
Learning reinforced through practicals, projects, and real-world application at every level.
A curriculum that accommodates different paces and styles so no child is left behind.
A Complete Education, from Start to Finish
A Curriculum Built for Every Student's Future
Regular class tests, assignments, and coursework contribute to each student's overall grade, rewarding consistent effort and engagement throughout the term.
Formal written examinations are conducted at the end of each term across all subjects, testing students' understanding and preparing them for national assessments.
Students at key stages sit national examinations set by the Ministry of Education — the PLE at primary level and the SSCSE at secondary level.
Structured mock exams are held before national assessments to familiarise students with exam conditions, identify gaps, and build the confidence needed to perform well.
Science and ICT subjects include practical components, assessed through supervised lab and computer sessions as part of the overall subject grade.
Language and humanities subjects may include oral examinations and presentations, developing students' communication skills alongside written ability.
South Sudan Certificate of Secondary Education
Dedicated revision timetables are provided before each exam period, with teachers running focused revision sessions covering key topics and exam techniques across all subjects.
Students practise with past national examination papers under timed conditions, helping them understand question formats and develop the pacing skills needed for exam success.
Teachers identify students who need additional support early and provide one-on-one guidance, targeted interventions, and extra practice to close gaps before the examination.
We keep parents informed throughout the exam cycle — sharing timetables, preparation tips, and student progress so that the home environment can support each student's efforts.
Marking aligned with national marking schemes
Scripts are marked and internally moderated for accuracy and consistency.
Grades are assigned and performance patterns identified to inform teaching.
Detailed reports are issued to parents with grades, comments, and teacher guidance.
Every Student Can Achieve Their Best
More Than Books — A Centre for Learning
A Space Built for Curious Minds
The Library is Open Monday to Friday
Science Comes Alive in Our Labs
A Purpose-Built Space for Scientific Discovery
Compound microscopes, biological models, specimen collections, dissection tools, and plant and animal study materials for life science practicals.
Glassware, reagents, Bunsen burners, precision balances, titration sets, and full safety equipment for chemical experiments.
Electrical circuit kits, magnetism and optics apparatus, Newton's cradles, measuring instruments, and wave demonstration equipment.
A range of precision measuring tools, data recording sheets, and scientific calculators to support accurate observation and analysis.
Lab coats, safety goggles, gloves, fire extinguishers, first aid kits, and proper ventilation systems across all workstations.
Practical guides, data tables, lab manuals, and curriculum reference sheets available at every workstation to support independent learning.
A Safe Environment for Every Experiment
Protective gear provided at every session
Qualified teacher supervision at all times
First aid and emergency procedures in place
Science is at the Heart of What We Do
Preparing Students for a Digital World
Internet access for research and learning
Hardware, software, operating systems, file management, and the basic skills every student needs to operate a computer confidently.
Document creation, formatting, editing, and presentation using word processing software — essential for academic and professional life.
Using spreadsheet applications for data entry, calculations, charts, and basic data analysis — a skill applied across all subjects.
Safe and effective use of the internet, search techniques, evaluating online sources, and email communication etiquette.
Understanding online safety, responsible digital citizenship, data privacy, and how to protect oneself in digital environments.
Basic programming logic, algorithms, and problem-solving using introductory coding concepts at the secondary level.
Technology as a Tool for Every Subject
Practical sessions tied to real-world applications
ICT integrated across the academic programme
Emphasis on responsible and safe technology use
Students are introduced to computers — basic operation, mouse and keyboard skills, and foundational digital literacy. Technology is explored through guided activities and simple tasks.
Students begin formal ICT studies — word processing, spreadsheets, internet research, and computer theory. Practical lab sessions reinforce classroom learning with hands-on exercises.
Students prepare for national examinations in ICT and deepen their skills in applications, networking concepts, digital communication, and introductory programming logic.
Technology is Part of Every Student's Future

Seven Simple Steps to Joining Our School
What to Bring — by School Level

The enrolment form, emergency contacts, parental agreement, and other paperwork are completed together with our admissions team on the day of registration — you do not need to prepare these in advance.
Fee amounts will be published shortly.
Please contact the admissions office directly for the current fee schedule. Fees are subject to review at the start of each academic year.
A non-refundable processing fee is charged at the time of submitting your application. This does not count toward term fees.
Fees must be paid in full
before a student can begin attending classes. Students with outstanding fees from a previous term may be asked to remain at home until the balance is cleared.
Fees are non-refundable once a student has attended class for the term. Partial refunds may be considered in exceptional circumstances at the administration's discretion.
are charged once at the time of enrolment. Replacement uniforms are purchased separately from the school's uniform supplier.
financial hardship or fee assistance enquiries
, please speak directly and confidentially with the school administration.
Clear, Honest Fees for Every Family

Arrange a guided tour of the school so you and your child can see facilities and meet the teaching team.
Come and See SLA in Person
A guided walk through our classrooms, library, science labs, sports grounds, and all key facilities.
An opportunity to meet our teachers, head teachers, and admissions staff and ask any questions you have.
A brief session covering the application process, fee structure, term dates, and enrolment requirements.
Time for open questions — about curriculum, school values, pastoral care, or anything else on your mind.
Visiting on a weekday gives you a chance to see the school in full operation — classrooms, teachers, and students all at work.
Speak with our admissions team before your visit.
